candidate.lever: Move existing batch_scan_add_emit_carry_i64 direct path before generic body-const, carry, and per-output scaffolding allocation while preserving the zero-length output guard.
candidate.decision: Source diff removed; no slower code kept.

behavior_proof.ordering: Accepted direct path would still emit row-major carry and y values, and reverse mode keeps input-order y values by writing each scan index position directly.
behavior_proof.tie_breaking: No comparisons or tie-breaking are introduced.
behavior_proof.floating_point: Target is I64-only; no FP operations are introduced.
behavior_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
behavior_proof.fallbacks: The direct-path recognizer and all existing guards were unchanged; nonmatching scan bodies would still use the generic path.
behavior_proof.rch_tests: cargo test -p fj-dispatch test_batch_eval_jaxpr_scan_sub_jaxprs -- --nocapture passed on ts1: 4 passed, 0 failed.

next_primitive: Do not repeat the scan scaffolding micro-lever. Next attack should use a different primitive from the post-pass119 profile, such as axis-1 vmap layout specialization or a linalg-local algorithmic kernel once ownership clears.
